title: MatchingAssessmentTypeValues
description:
All key attributes of a transaction must match exactly with the reference data.

Comment:
Example: Check number, amount, and payee match exactly with the issued record → Approved automatically.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Tolerance Match
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Allows small, predefined discrepancies between the transaction and reference data.

Comment:
Example: Invoice amount differs by ±$5 from purchase order → Still considered a match.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Duplicate Check
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Verifies that a transaction or payment has not already been processed.

Comment:
Example: Same check number or payment ID presented twice → Flagged as duplicate.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Conditional Match
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Matching is based on specific business rules or conditions beyond simple exact or tolerance checks.

Comment:
Example: Payment approved if payee and account match, even if amount differs within tolerance.
